upstream.commit_pinned_note: Reference commit captured 2026-05-19 from kunminghu-v3 HEAD via `gh api repos/OpenXiangShan/XiangShan/commits/kunminghu-v3`; refresh requires re-running scripts/check_branch_prediction.py and updating both manifest and docs/arch/branch-prediction.md before claims advance.

selected_path.not_phone_class_reason: XiangShan Kunminghu has been taped out on TSMC N28/N14 at 1-2 GHz. A 2028 phone-class flagship AP requires N3/14A-class timing closure, dual-issue taken-branch BPU, FDIP-fed L1I, AOSP/Android workload MPKI evidence, and silicon validation that the open IP has not yet demonstrated.

deviations: Per-table TAGE useful-bit reset alternates LSB/MSB on a fixed cycle period rather than tracking misprediction-driven heuristics. This is the Seznec CBP-5 baseline; KMH uses a more aggressive adaptive scheme.
ITTAGE storage uses uniform `ITTAGE_ENTRIES_MAX`-sized arrays with index hashing modulo the per-table entry count. Synthesised area follows the largest table; KMH packs tables tightly.
Two-taken-per-cycle FTB read is parameterised but the prediction pipeline currently only emits one taken branch per cycle. Lifting to two taken requires a follow-up RTL workstream.
FDIP-style L1I prefetch is wired as a hint only; the prefetch engine remains a downstream blocker.

claim_policy.reason: Open topology is wired in synthesisable SystemVerilog and exercised by cocotb. Workload MPKI claims remain blocked until SPEC/AOSP/JS traces are ingested through benchmarks/cpu/branch/.
